Detection source: Input reverse-protection controller and protected rail absence
Firmware response: Keep all outputs disabled and log reverse-input fault
Safety constraint: No downstream rail may energize under reverse polarity

Fault condition: Load dump or surge above normal input range
Detection source: TVS clamp review and voltage telemetry
Hardware default: Clamp path absorbs transient within selected device limits
Firmware response: Disable or hold outputs in safe state and log surge event if telemetry survives
Safety constraint: Clamp must protect controller MOSFET buck and monitor absolute maximum ratings

Firmware response: Hold outputs disabled block startup until power-good is valid and log logic power fault
Safety constraint: Outputs must remain off when logic rail is invalid

Detection source: LB_3V3_IO absent and control nets pulled low
Hardware default: All OUTn control inputs remain off
Firmware response: Do not attempt output enable and log board-interface unavailable if firmware runs
Safety constraint: PB-100 must fail safe without valid LB-100 control

Detection source: TPS48110 current warning or current fault
Hardware default: Controller faults or limits output according to schematic values
Firmware response: Disable affected OUTn apply lockout policy and log overcurrent
Safety constraint: Fault handling must remain per-output and role-agnostic

Detection source: TPS48110 short-circuit threshold and fuse behavior
Hardware default: Controller turns off or fuse isolates fault path
Firmware response: Disable affected OUTn require explicit recovery policy and log short fault
Safety constraint: Fault energy must remain inside MOSFET controller and fuse SOA

Fault condition: OUT2 startup pulse exceeds validated envelope
Detection source: OUT2 current telemetry and controller timing
Hardware default: Controller timer and SOA limits protect MOSFET
Firmware response: Block or retry OUT2 only under reviewed policy and log inrush fault
Safety constraint: OUT2 behavior must not exceed validated SOA envelope

Fault condition: User-serviceable output fuse open or missing
Detection source: OUTn voltage/current mismatch and fault status
Firmware response: Disable affected OUTn report unavailable and log fuse fault
Safety constraint: Open fuse must not be bypassed by firmware or alternate path

Fault condition: PCB or power-zone temperature exceeds derating threshold
Hardware default: Hardware remains within selected thermal assumptions
Firmware response: Derate shed or disable outputs by priority and log thermal fault
Safety constraint: Derating must occur before unsafe component temperature

Fault condition: Thermal telemetry missing stale or implausible
Firmware response: Derate or disable affected output groups and log thermal telemetry fault
Safety constraint: Missing thermal data must not be treated as safe temperature

Fault condition: Current telemetry missing stale or implausible
Hardware default: Hardware current limits and fuses remain primary protection
Firmware response: Disable affected output or reduce current budget and log telemetry fault
Safety constraint: Missing current data must not allow uncontrolled load operation

Fault condition: Requested load exceeds board current budget
Detection source: Firmware budget calculation and IIN_SENSE feedback
Hardware default: No hardware path overrides fuse and controller limits
Firmware response: Refuse startup or shed lower-priority outputs and log budget fault
Safety constraint: Board current budget must be enforced independently of accessory role names

Fault condition: CAN1 TX route populated or disable status not asserted
Firmware response: Treat CAN1 as listen-only block TX and log safety fault
Safety constraint: Vehicle CAN TX requires future ADR and explicit hardware action

Fault condition: PB-100 board identity or pin-map mismatch
Hardware default: Outputs remain off until capability is accepted
Firmware response: Reject configuration for unknown board keep outputs disabled and log identity fault
Safety constraint: Hardware capability discovery must stay role-agnostic and must not use accessory role assumptions
